qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SOAP API authentication bypass via session cookie cross-user impersonation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable code and root cause: this finding targets SOAP mci_check_login cookie validation and auth_attempt_script_login password skipping, while the advisory targets unescaped Timeline rendering of history tag names in IssueTagTimelineEvent::html().

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described stored HTML injection/XSS root cause: tag names retrieved from history are rendered in Timeline without string_html_specialchars() in IssueTagTimelineEvent::html().
evidence: Advisory GHSA-73vx-49mv-v8w5 describes improper escaping of renamed or deleted tag names when displayed in Timeline on my_view_page.php, with the workaround to wrap $this->tag_name in string_html_specialchars() in IssueTagTimelineEvent::html().
Final qualifying finding soap_auth_bypass describes an unrelated SOAP API authentication bypass in api/soap/mc_api.php and core/authentication_api.php.
The final non-qualifying tag_detach_missing_auth finding concerns REST tag detach authorization checks and does not identify the Timeline tag-name rendering sink.
